candidates.title: Demo Artifact Index
candidates.source: The paper demo (scripts/demo_paper_workflow.sh) produces a temporary workspace, config, discipline profile, backtest report, and audit directory — but there is no single indexed view of what each artifact proves or where it lives. Reviewers must read the script and multiple docs to reconstruct the artifact map.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: A docs page lists every artifact the demo produces, its path, its content summary, and the safety invariant it demonstrates. Cross-referenced from README and external-reviewer-walkthrough.
candidates.reason: Highest-impact demo auditability improvement. Docs-only; no runtime changes.

candidates.title: Demo Proof Checker
candidates.source: tests/test_demo_proof_layer.py validates static script safety (forbidden phrases, secrets, shebang) but does not verify that demo docs and script remain consistent, or that expected artifacts are documented. A deterministic checker would close this gap without executing the demo.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates that demo docs list all script commands, that expected artifacts match script outputs, that symbols are consistent across docs, and that no over-promise claims exist in demo surfaces. Runs locally without credentials or network.
candidates.reason: Prevents doc-script drift and gives reviewers a one-command verification path. No runtime changes.

candidates.title: Reviewer Demo Path Consolidation
candidates.source: Swarm safety audit found three over-promise issues in public-facing docs: (1) docs/trust/README.md line 83 claims Prepared v0.6.8 release notes and release status documentation — no such docs exist; (2) README.md line 179 claims The 0.6.8 source version on main is prepared — source is 0.6.7; (3) docs/brokers.md line 12 calls PaperBroker production-ready — ambiguous wording for a safety-first framework. Additionally, the README quickstart uses DEMO-SYMBOL while the demo script uses ATLAS-DEMO, causing confusion.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Over-promise claims are corrected; symbol usage is unified or explicitly explained; duplicate demo instructions across README/walkthrough/demo-doc are consolidated or cross-referenced with canonical pointers; reviewer path is one clean narrative.
candidates.reason: Directly fixes reviewer trust issues discovered by swarm. Docs-only; no runtime changes.

candidates.title: Demo command smoke validation under smoke/local quick tier
candidates.source: The smoke and local quick check tiers validate core safety but do not assert that the demo script exists, is executable, and contains expected commands. Adding a lightweight validation would catch accidental demo script breakage early.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Smoke and local quick tiers include a fast demo-script existence and structure check (reusing logic from test_demo_proof_layer.py or check_demo_proof.py if implemented).
candidates.reason: Small, coherent extension of CAND-002 into the tier system. Keeps demo integrity in the fastest gates.

candidates.title: Backtest report schema contract
candidates.source: tests/backtest/test_backtest_reports.py tests rendering but no JSON schema documents the result.json structure. The render_json_report function injects extra keys (generated_at, disclaimer, report_type) not on BacktestResult, creating a latent Pydantic round-trip mismatch. A schema contract would close the v0.6.3 roadmap gap for JSON schema tests.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Documented schema for result.json; deterministic validation test that engine run → report → schema validation passes; no runtime execution changes.
candidates.reason: Good candidate but extends beyond the public-demo-proof theme. Defer to v0.7.x backtest batch.

candidates.title: Provider preflight deterministic checker
candidates.source: Provider preflight is mature (1026 lines of tests, CI-backed) but there is no single scripts/check_provider_preflight.py for reviewers to verify docs, artifact schemas, and import safety without reading all code.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates preflight docs exist, AST-scans for forbidden imports, and optionally runs smoke chain into temp dir to validate expected artifacts.
candidates.reason: Good candidate but extends beyond the public-demo-proof theme. Defer to v0.6.9 or v0.7.x provider batch.

candidates.title: Dashboard validation docs polish
candidates.source: The dashboard is fully implemented (runtime HTML generation, tested, ~1000 lines). docs/dashboard.md is only 43 lines and lacks usage examples. No code changes are needed.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Expanded usage examples and What you'll see section.
candidates.reason: Docs-only but out of demo-proof theme. Defer to v0.7.x dashboard batch.

rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.reason: Skill candidates remain manually reviewed and offline.

safety_boundaries: Live trading remains disabled by default.
Live submit remains disabled by default.
Provider execution remains disabled by default.
Broker execution remains disabled by default.
Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.
Human approval remains required for live order flow.
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured.
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
constraints: Version is not bumped to 0.6.8 in pyproject.toml and src/atlas_agent/__init__.py
scripts/check_v068_release_prep.py and matching tests do not exist yet
No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
release_criteria: dev_check, ci_check, and release_check --quick pass locally
CI on main is green after the prep commit is pushed

scripts/check_v068_release_prep.py and matching tests are created
